title: Graphs - things and how they connect
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A graph is a set of nodes connected by edges. It models anything about relationships - maps, networks, who-follows-whom. A common way to store it is an adjacency list: each node mapped to its neighbours.
explanation: A tree was a graph with rules (one root, no loops). A graph drops the rules: any node can connect to any other, loops allowed, and an edge can run one way or both. That generality is why graphs model the messy real world - roads, friendships, dependencies. The everyday representation is an adjacency list: a dictionary from each node to the list of nodes it links to. Cheap to store, and exactly what you walk when you explore it.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: The list stored under the key "a".
